content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be repurposed as a shared distributed memory layer for A2A (Agent-to-Agent) communication protocols, where agents actively query only the most uncertain unlabeled memory fragments from the collective agent memory pool instead of synchronizing entire full memory stores. This turns traditional active learning's goal of selecting data for an individual agent's self-improvement into a mechanism for reducing redundant communication across the entire A2A network.
